enrichment.short_description: OpsLevel is an internal developer portal with a software catalog, standards Scorecards, and self-service actions for engineering teams.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: OpsLevel: internal developer portal with a software catalog, Scorecards, Checks, and self-service actions for engineering teams.

features.title: Unified software catalog
features.description: Centralizes services, infrastructure, and ownership information into a single, browsable catalog with AI-powered catalog enrichment.

features.title: Scorecards and Checks
features.description: Automated Checks and Scorecards let teams define and enforce engineering standards and track service health over time.

features.title: Self-service actions and templates
features.description: Developers can trigger service templates and self-service actions directly from the catalog instead of filing tickets.

features.title: Knowledge Center
features.description: Centralizes API references and technical documentation alongside catalog entries.

features.title: Maintenance Agent
features.description: An AI Maintenance Agent is designed to automate code maintenance work at scale.

features.title: Role-based access control
features.description: Granular RBAC controls who can view or modify catalog data and configuration.

features.title: 60+ integrations
features.description: Connects with tools including GitHub, Kubernetes, AWS, Azure, Google Cloud, Slack, PagerDuty, New Relic, Datadog, ServiceNow, Jira, Snyk, and Veracode.

pros: Wide integration ecosystem covering 60+ tools
Combines catalog, standards enforcement, and self-service actions in one platform
AI-powered catalog enrichment and a Maintenance Agent aim to reduce manual catalog upkeep
Guided product tours let prospects explore the product without signing up
cons: Pricing is not published; requires contacting sales for a quote
No self-serve free trial — access requires booking a demo
Priced per developer, so cost can rise with headcount

faqs.question: Does OpsLevel offer a free trial?
faqs.answer: OpsLevel does not list a self-serve free trial. Instead it offers guided product tours and scheduled demos before granting testing access.

faqs.question: How is OpsLevel priced?
faqs.answer: OpsLevel is priced based on the number of developers using the portal, with volume discounts, quoted via a Standard or Enterprise plan.

faqs.question: What's the difference between the Standard and Enterprise plans?
faqs.answer: Standard supports up to 50 users. Enterprise offers unlimited users plus a customizable UI, on-premises/single-tenancy deployment, a dedicated customer success manager, and beta feature access.

faqs.question: What integrations does OpsLevel support?
faqs.answer: Over 60 tools, including GitHub, Kubernetes, AWS, Azure, Google Cloud, Slack, PagerDuty, New Relic, Datadog, ServiceNow, Jira, Snyk, and Veracode.

use_cases.title: Service ownership and catalog management
use_cases.description: Platform teams use OpsLevel to maintain a single source of truth for every microservice, its owners, and its dependencies.

use_cases.title: Enforcing engineering standards
use_cases.description: Scorecards and Checks let engineering leaders track and enforce standards such as security, reliability, and documentation compliance across services.

use_cases.title: Developer self-service
use_cases.description: Developers use self-service actions and templates to scaffold new services without waiting on platform teams.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is OpsLevel?
paragraphs: OpsLevel is an internal developer portal built around a software catalog that gives engineering teams a single place to track services, ownership, and infrastructure.

The platform adds Scorecards and Checks for enforcing engineering standards, self-service actions and templates for scaffolding, and a Knowledge Center for API and technical documentation, alongside AI-powered catalog enrichment and a Maintenance Agent.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who OpsLevel is for
paragraphs: OpsLevel targets platform engineers, site reliability engineers, software developers, and engineering leaders managing microservice architectures who need visibility into service ownership and health, plus integrations across 60+ tools such as GitHub, Kubernetes, AWS, PagerDuty, and Datadog.

enrichment.short_description: Roadie is a managed Backstage software catalog and developer portal that also exposes a live context graph for AI agents.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Roadie: a managed Backstage developer portal with a software catalog, TechDocs, and a live context graph for AI agents.

features.title: Backstage software catalog
features.description: A managed, automatically-upgraded Backstage software catalog for tracking services, teams, and APIs.

features.title: TechDocs and API specs
features.description: Built-in TechDocs and API specification support for centralized technical documentation.

features.title: Self-service templates
features.description: Software templates let teams scaffold new services and standardize creation workflows.

features.title: 75+ plugins
features.description: Access to more than 75 Backstage plugins for extending the portal without self-hosting Backstage.

features.title: Dynamic context graph
features.description: Links services, teams, APIs, and infrastructure, pulling live data from tools like Prometheus, Kubernetes, PagerDuty, GitHub, and LaunchDarkly.

features.title: MCP server and API for agents
features.description: Exposes an MCP server and REST API so AI agents can query engineering context on demand.

features.title: SSO and RAG AI search
features.description: Includes single sign-on and retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) AI search across catalog content.

pros: Fully managed Backstage distribution with automatic upgrades
Access to 75+ Backstage plugins without operating Backstage yourself
Purpose-built context graph and MCP server for AI agent workflows
SOC 2 Type 2 certified
cons: No published free plan or trial
The $24/developer/month Teams plan is limited to existing subscribers
Growth and Enterprise Context plan pricing is not public and requires contacting sales

faqs.question: Is Roadie based on Backstage?
faqs.answer: Yes, Roadie's Developer Portal plans are built on a managed Backstage software catalog with automatic upgrades and access to 75+ plugins.

faqs.question: Does Roadie offer a free plan?
faqs.answer: No free plan or trial is listed on Roadie's pricing page. The Teams plan starts at $24 per developer/month and is limited to existing subscribers, with Growth and Enterprise Context priced individually.

faqs.question: What is Roadie's context graph?
faqs.answer: A dynamic context graph linking services, teams, APIs, and infrastructure using live data from tools like Prometheus, Kubernetes, PagerDuty, GitHub, and LaunchDarkly, exposed via an MCP server and API for AI agents.

faqs.question: Is Roadie SOC 2 certified?
faqs.answer: Yes, Roadie states it is SOC 2 Type 2 certified.

use_cases.title: Managed Backstage developer portal
use_cases.description: Engineering teams get a hosted, auto-updating Backstage catalog with TechDocs and templates without operating Backstage themselves.

use_cases.title: AI agent context
use_cases.description: Teams give AI coding and on-call agents structured, live context about services and infrastructure via Roadie's context graph and MCP server.
use_cases.audience: Platform and SRE teams adopting AI agents

use_cases.title: Standardizing service creation
use_cases.description: Self-service software templates and 75+ plugins let teams standardize how new services are scaffolded.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Roadie?
paragraphs: Roadie provides a managed, automatically-upgraded Backstage software catalog with TechDocs, API specs, self-service templates, and access to 75+ Backstage plugins.

It also exposes a dynamic context graph — linking services, teams, APIs, and infrastructure with live data from tools like Prometheus, Kubernetes, PagerDuty, GitHub, and LaunchDarkly — through an MCP server and API so AI agents can query engineering context on demand.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Roadie is for
paragraphs: Roadie is built for engineering and platform teams that want a hosted Backstage catalog without running Backstage themselves, and organizations giving AI agents structured, live context on their production systems.

name: Compass
skip_reason: Atlassian is discontinuing Compass: end-of-sale was May 13, 2026 (already past as of this review), renewals end December 31, 2026, and Compass's scorecard/catalog functionality is being transitioned into a separate product called DX, with support concluding by December 2027. Publishing fresh editorial content for a product already in wind-down would be misleading for a directory.

name: Gitness
skip_reason: gitness.com now 301-redirects to harness.io/open-source. The standalone "Gitness" product/brand has been folded into "Harness Open Source"; "Gitness" now only survives as the name of a sub-feature (hosted Cloud Development Environments) within that rebranded product. This is a name mismatch/rebrand versus the manifest entry, so it was not enriched under the old name.

enrichment.short_description: Gitea is a free, MIT-licensed, self-hosted Git service with built-in CI/CD, issue tracking, and package registry support.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Gitea: free, MIT-licensed self-hosted Git service with Gitea Actions CI/CD, packages, plus an optional paid Cloud/Enterprise tier.

features.title: Git repository hosting
features.description: Git repository creation and management with code review capabilities.

features.title: Gitea Actions
features.description: A built-in CI/CD system that is closely compatible with GitHub Actions.

features.title: Project management
features.description: Issue tracking, kanban boards, and progress tracking tools.

features.title: Package registry
features.description: Support for more than 20 package management formats, including NPM, PyPI, and Maven.

features.title: Integrations
features.description: API and webhook support, with compatibility for tools like Slack, Discord, and Jenkins.

features.title: Cross-platform deployment
features.description: Runs on Linux, Windows, macOS, FreeBSD, and Kubernetes.

pros: Free, MIT-licensed, self-hosted with unlimited users and repositories
Built-in CI/CD (Gitea Actions) compatible with GitHub Actions syntax
Supports 20+ package registry formats
Deployable on Linux, Windows, macOS, FreeBSD, and Kubernetes
cons: Enterprise support and features require a paid subscription starting at $9.50/user/month with a one-year commitment
Managed Gitea Cloud pricing beyond the 30-day trial is not published on the pages reviewed
Self-hosting requires operating and maintaining your own infrastructure

faqs.question: Is Gitea free?
faqs.answer: Yes, the core self-hosted Gitea service is free under the MIT license with unlimited users and repositories.

faqs.question: Does Gitea offer CI/CD?
faqs.answer: Yes, Gitea Actions is a built-in CI/CD system closely compatible with GitHub Actions.

faqs.question: Is there a hosted version of Gitea?
faqs.answer: Yes, Gitea Cloud offers managed hosting with a 30-day free trial.

faqs.question: What does Gitea Enterprise cost?
faqs.answer: Gitea Enterprise starts at $9.50 per user/month with a one-year commitment.

use_cases.title: Self-hosted source control
use_cases.description: Organizations wanting full control of Git hosting on their own infrastructure deploy Gitea instead of using a SaaS host.

use_cases.title: Lightweight CI/CD alongside Git
use_cases.description: Teams use Gitea Actions to run builds and tests directly next to their repositories.

use_cases.title: Centralized package management
use_cases.description: Teams centralize NPM, PyPI, Maven, and other packages alongside their code in Gitea's package registry.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Gitea?
paragraphs: Gitea is a self-hosted DevOps platform combining Git repository hosting with code review, Gitea Actions CI/CD compatible with GitHub Actions, issue tracking and kanban boards, and a package registry supporting more than 20 formats.

The core self-hosted product is free and MIT-licensed with unlimited users and repositories. A managed Gitea Cloud option offers a 30-day free trial, and Gitea Enterprise adds paid support and features starting at $9.50/user/month.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Gitea is for
paragraphs: Gitea suits teams and developers who want a lightweight, self-hosted alternative to GitHub/GitLab, deployable on Linux, Windows, macOS, FreeBSD, or Kubernetes, with an optional paid Cloud or Enterprise tier for organizations that need managed hosting or dedicated support.

enrichment.short_description: Forgejo is a free, self-hosted, lightweight software forge governed by the non-profit Codeberg e.V.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Forgejo: free, open source, lightweight self-hosted software forge governed by the non-profit Codeberg e.V.

features.title: Lightweight self-hosting
features.description: Designed to require an order of magnitude less resources than other software forges.

features.title: GitHub-like interface
features.description: A familiar interface for teams transitioning from GitHub to a self-hosted solution.

features.title: Federation focus
features.description: Development prioritizes security, scaling, federation, and privacy.

features.title: Community governance
features.description: Developed under the umbrella of Codeberg e.V., a democratic non-profit organization, with contribution teams for localization, code, design, documentation, UX, and community management.

features.title: Codeberg hosting option
features.description: Users can create an account on Codeberg instead of self-hosting their own Forgejo instance.

pros: Committed to remaining free and open source ("Forgejo will always be Free and Open Source Software")
Minimal server resource requirements compared to other forges
Governed by a non-profit (Codeberg e.V.) rather than a commercial vendor
Option to use the hosted Codeberg service instead of self-hosting
cons: No commercial support tier or SLA mentioned on the official site
No managed cloud/paid offering from the Forgejo project itself
Self-hosting requires operational expertise unless using the community-run Codeberg service

faqs.question: Is Forgejo free?
faqs.answer: Yes, Forgejo states it "will always be Free and Open Source Software" with no paid tiers.

faqs.question: Who governs Forgejo?
faqs.answer: Forgejo operates under the umbrella of Codeberg e.V., a democratic non-profit organization, with development coordinated via Codeberg and Matrix chat.

faqs.question: Can I use Forgejo without self-hosting?
faqs.answer: Yes, users can create an account on Codeberg instead of self-hosting their own instance.

faqs.question: What kind of resources does Forgejo need?
faqs.answer: Forgejo describes itself as requiring an order of magnitude less resources than other forges, making it suitable for lightweight self-hosting.

use_cases.title: Self-hosted forge for small teams
use_cases.description: Teams with limited server resources use Forgejo for a low-maintenance, lightweight self-hosted forge.

use_cases.title: Non-profit-governed hosting
use_cases.description: Organizations wanting a non-commercial governance model can rely on Forgejo's stewardship under Codeberg e.V., or host directly on Codeberg.
use_cases.audience: Open source projects and privacy-conscious teams

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Forgejo?
paragraphs: Forgejo is a self-hosted, lightweight software forge with a GitHub-like interface, built to run on significantly fewer server resources than comparable forges.
The project is entirely free and open source and is governed by Codeberg e.V., a democratic non-profit organization, with contributions coordinated through Codeberg and Matrix chat.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Forgejo is for
paragraphs: Forgejo suits developers and teams who want a low-maintenance, self-hosted Git forge, or who prefer a non-profit-governed project over a commercially owned one; those who don't want to self-host can use the Codeberg-hosted instance instead.

enrichment.short_description: RhodeCode is enterprise source code management unifying Git, Mercurial, and Subversion, with a free Community Edition and paid Enterprise/Cloud tiers.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: RhodeCode: source code management for Git, Mercurial, and Subversion with a free Community Edition and paid Enterprise/Cloud plans.

features.title: Multi-VCS support
features.description: Centralized control for Git, Mercurial, and Subversion repositories under one platform.

features.title: Code review and collaboration
features.description: Built-in code review tools with workflow automation and issue tracker integrations.

features.title: Unified permissions
features.description: Fine-grained, unified permission management across multiple VCS platforms.

features.title: Enterprise authentication
features.description: LDAP+Groups, SAML, AD, CAS, Google, OAuth, and 2FA support on Enterprise and Cloud plans.

features.title: High availability
features.description: Multi-node configurations for scalable, highly available deployments.

features.title: Behind-the-firewall deployment
features.description: On-premises deployment option with IP restrictions for enterprise-grade security.

pros: Free, open source Community Edition with unlimited users
Supports Git, Mercurial, and Subversion on a single platform
Cloud and Enterprise tiers add SSO, high availability, and support with an SLA
Cloud plan includes unlimited traffic, disk space, and repository size and is cancelable anytime
cons: Enterprise and Cloud pricing is sold in minimum 10-user packs
Community Edition lacks enterprise authentication (SAML, AD, 2FA) and HA clustering
Multi-VCS support beyond Git is a less common requirement for most modern teams

faqs.question: Is RhodeCode free?
faqs.answer: The RhodeCode Community Edition is free and open source with unlimited users. Enterprise ($75/user/year) and Cloud (from $8/user/month) are paid tiers.

faqs.question: What version control systems does RhodeCode support?
faqs.answer: Git, Mercurial, and Subversion under a single platform.

faqs.question: Is there a free trial?
faqs.answer: RhodeCode mentions a 30-day trial for both its on-premises and cloud offerings.

faqs.question: What's included in RhodeCode Cloud?
faqs.answer: Dedicated isolated hardware, enterprise SSL, unlimited traffic/disk/repo size, geo-located instances (US, Asia, Europe), daily backups, and monitoring, starting at $8/user/month for a minimum of 10 users.

use_cases.title: Consolidating mixed VCS repositories
use_cases.description: Organizations running a mix of Git, Mercurial, and Subversion repositories use RhodeCode to manage them under one unified platform.

use_cases.title: Behind-the-firewall source control
use_cases.description: Enterprises requiring on-premises deployment with IP restrictions use RhodeCode Enterprise for regulated environments.

features: LDAP+Groups, SAML, AD, CAS, Google, OAuth, 2FA
Unlimited traffic, disk, and repo size

long_form_blocks.heading: What is RhodeCode?
paragraphs: RhodeCode is enterprise source code management that provides centralized control for Git, Mercurial, and Subversion repositories under a single platform, with code review, workflow automation, and unified permission management.
It ships as a free, open source Community Edition, plus a paid Enterprise Edition ($75/user/year) for on-premises deployments and a Cloud offering (from $8/user/month) for managed, dedicated hosting.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who RhodeCode is for
paragraphs: RhodeCode targets enterprises and regulated organizations that need to consolidate multiple version control systems, enforce enterprise authentication, or deploy source control behind their own firewall.

enrichment.short_description: Gogs is a free, MIT-licensed, self-hosted Git service written in Go that runs on minimal hardware.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Gogs: a free, open source, self-hosted Git service written in Go, MIT-licensed since 2014, runs on minimal hardware.

features.title: Simple installation
features.description: Run the binary directly, or run Gogs as a Docker container.

features.title: Cross-platform
features.description: Runs on any platform supported by Go, including Linux, macOS, Windows, and ARM devices.

features.title: Minimal resource requirements
features.description: Functions efficiently with as little as 64 MB of RAM and a quarter vCPU.

features.title: Open source
features.description: MIT-licensed with all source code publicly available since 2014.

pros: Extremely lightweight; runs on as little as 64 MB RAM and a quarter vCPU
Simple binary or Docker-based installation
Free and MIT-licensed
Cross-platform (Linux, macOS, Windows, ARM)
cons: No documented CI/CD or package registry features on the official site
No hosted/cloud offering — self-hosting only
No paid tier for dedicated support

faqs.question: Is Gogs free?
faqs.answer: Yes, Gogs is MIT-licensed and free, with source code publicly available since 2014.

faqs.question: What are Gogs' hardware requirements?
faqs.answer: Gogs can run with as little as 64 MB of RAM and a quarter vCPU.

faqs.question: What platforms does Gogs support?
faqs.answer: Any platform supported by Go, including Linux, macOS, Windows, and ARM devices.

faqs.question: How do I install Gogs?
faqs.answer: Run the binary directly, or run it as a Docker container.

use_cases.title: Self-hosting Git on minimal hardware
use_cases.description: Teams with constrained infrastructure, such as a small VPS or single-board computer, use Gogs to run a Git service on very limited resources.

use_cases.title: Lightweight private Git hosting
use_cases.description: Developers wanting a simple, low-maintenance private Git server without cloud dependency use Gogs.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Gogs?
paragraphs: Gogs is a self-hosted Git service written in Go, designed to be a simple, stable, and extensible way to run your own Git repositories.

It installs as a single binary or Docker container, runs on any platform Go supports, and is engineered to work with as little as 64 MB of RAM and a quarter vCPU. It has been MIT-licensed and publicly available since 2014.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Gogs is for
paragraphs: Gogs is aimed at individuals, hobbyists, and small teams who want a minimal, self-hosted Git server that can run on very limited hardware.

enrichment.short_description: SourceHut is an open source software forge offering Git/Mercurial hosting, CI, mailing lists, and ticket tracking.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: SourceHut: open source software forge with Git/Mercurial hosting, CI, mailing lists, and ticket tracking; hosted plans from $4/month.

features.title: Git and Mercurial hosting
features.description: Repository hosting with fine-grained access control for both Git and Mercurial.

features.title: Continuous integration
features.description: Runs fully virtualized builds across various Linux distributions and BSDs.

features.title: Mailing lists and code review
features.description: Mailing lists and code review powered by git send-email.

features.title: Ticket tracking
features.description: A ticket tracker focused on actionable tasks only.

features.title: Hosted IRC
features.description: Hosted IRC chat services with bouncer functionality.

features.title: Git-driven wikis
features.description: Wiki hosting driven directly by Git repositories.

features.title: Paste and static web hosting
features.description: Includes paste hosting and static website hosting.

features.title: hut CLI
features.description: A CLI tool for data export and management across SourceHut services.

pros: 100% free and open source software (AGPL, BSD, and other licenses)
Self-hostable as an alternative to the paid hosted service
States no tracking, no advertising, and no AI features
Financial aid available for users whose situation can't support the subscription fee
Free to use for contributing to existing projects on the hosted service
cons: Using most hosted services as an owner (e.g. builds.sr.ht) requires a paid subscription ($4-$12/month)
Email/mailing-list-based workflows (git send-email code review) have a learning curve versus GitHub-style pull requests
Explicitly excludes AI features, which some teams may want

faqs.question: Is SourceHut free?
faqs.answer: SourceHut is free to self-host and free for contributing to existing projects on the hosted service. Using most hosted services as an owner requires a paid subscription starting at $4/month.

faqs.question: What version control systems does SourceHut support?
faqs.answer: Git and Mercurial repository hosting.

faqs.question: Does SourceHut have AI features?
faqs.answer: No — the site states "No AI features whatsoever."

faqs.question: Where is SourceHut based?
faqs.answer: Its listed address is Postbus 3068, 1620GB Hoorn, Netherlands.

faqs.question: Is financial aid available?
faqs.answer: Yes, SourceHut states it never asks for payment from anyone whose financial situation would not comfortably support the subscription fee, and users can apply for assistance.

use_cases.title: Hosting open source projects
use_cases.description: Open source and niche-language projects use SourceHut's integrated Git hosting, CI, mailing lists, and ticket tracking.

use_cases.title: Privacy-focused development workflow
use_cases.description: Developers who want no tracking, no ads, and no AI features use SourceHut for a minimal, privacy-respecting toolchain.

use_cases.title: Self-hosted software forge
use_cases.description: Teams that prefer to self-host rather than pay for the hosted service can run their own SourceHut instance.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is SourceHut?
paragraphs: SourceHut, described by its creators as "the hacker's forge," is a fully open source software development platform combining Git and Mercurial hosting, virtualized CI, mailing-list-based code review, a ticket tracker, hosted IRC, Git-driven wikis, and paste/static web hosting.

The hosted service is free for contributing to existing projects, while owning projects on most services requires a paid subscription starting at $4/month. The entire platform can also be self-hosted, and it states no tracking, no advertising, and no AI features.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who SourceHut is for
paragraphs: SourceHut is aimed at developers and open source communities who want an integrated, privacy-respecting toolchain and are comfortable with an email- and CLI-centric workflow rather than a GitHub-style pull request UI.

enrichment.short_description: OneDev is a self-hosted DevOps platform unifying Git, issues, pull requests, CI/CD, and packages, with a free Community Edition.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: OneDev: self-hosted Git, CI/CD, and issue tracking platform with a free Community Edition and Enterprise Edition from $6/user/month.

features.title: Unified DevOps platform
features.description: Combines Git, issues, pull requests, CI/CD, packages, and workspaces in one place.

features.title: Code search and navigation
features.description: Per-commit code search with language-aware symbol detection.

features.title: Built-in CI/CD
features.description: CI/CD as code with a GUI editor and multiple executor types, including containers, Kubernetes, agents, and bare metal.

features.title: Package registries
features.description: Built-in registries supporting Docker, NPM, Maven, NuGet, PyPI, RubyGems, and Helm.

features.title: Automated Kanban boards
features.description: Task management with customizable issue workflows.

features.title: Service desk
features.description: Email-to-issue linking for support and request handling.

features.title: AI user automation
features.description: AI integration described as supporting autonomous development work.

pros: Free Community Edition includes built-in CI/CD, package registries, code search, SSO/2FA, and LDAP/AD support
Combines Git hosting, issue tracking, CI/CD, and packages in a single self-hosted platform
Enterprise Edition adds HA/scaling, security scanning, and audit logging for $6/user/month
Includes AI user automation for autonomous development work
cons: Enterprise Edition requires a minimum order of 12 user-months
Security/compliance scanning, cross-project search, and HA clustering are Enterprise-only, not in the free Community Edition
Self-hosted only — no managed cloud offering found on the official site

faqs.question: Is OneDev free?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Community Edition is free and includes a built-in CI/CD engine, package registries, code search, and SSO/2FA.

faqs.question: What does OneDev Enterprise cost?
faqs.answer: $6 per user/month, with a minimum order of 12 user-months.

faqs.question: What package formats does OneDev support?
faqs.answer: Docker, NPM, Maven, NuGet, PyPI, RubyGems, and Helm.

faqs.question: Does OneDev include CI/CD?
faqs.answer: Yes, a built-in CI/CD engine with a GUI editor and multiple executor types: containers, Kubernetes, agents, and bare metal.

faqs.question: Does OneDev have AI features?
faqs.answer: The site lists AI integration for autonomous development work and AI user automation among its included capabilities.

use_cases.title: All-in-one self-hosted DevOps
use_cases.description: Teams wanting Git, issues, CI/CD, and packages in one self-hosted tool use OneDev instead of stitching together separate systems.

use_cases.title: Regulated/enterprise self-hosting
use_cases.description: Organizations needing high availability, security scanning, and audit logging upgrade to the OneDev Enterprise Edition.

features: Package registry (Docker, NPM, Maven, NuGet, PyPI, RubyGems, Helm)

long_form_blocks.heading: What is OneDev?
paragraphs: OneDev is a self-hosted DevOps platform that unifies Git repository hosting, issue tracking with automated Kanban boards, pull requests, built-in CI/CD, and package registries in one place.

The free Community Edition covers core Git, CI/CD, and package registry functionality, while the paid Enterprise Edition ($6/user/month, 12 user-month minimum) adds high availability, security and compliance scanning, cross-project search, and audit logging.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who OneDev is for
paragraphs: OneDev suits teams that want to self-host a single platform covering source control, issues, and CI/CD rather than combining separate tools, with an Enterprise tier for organizations that need HA, compliance scanning, and audit logging.
